title: Family of Stephenson King Jr., the man killed by Boston police officer now charged with manslaughter, demands transparency
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Family of Stephenson King Jr. demands release of body-camera footage after his shooting by Boston officer on March 11.
Officer Nicholas O'Malley charged with manslaughter, pleads not guilty; defense claims he acted in duty confronting a criminal.
Attorney Ben Crump represents King's family, emphasizing need for transparency and accountability in the incident.

title: Her first marathon is for her dad and grandfather — lifelong South Boston residents
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Olivia Dennett, 22, will run her first Boston Marathon for South Boston Neighborhood House, honoring her late father and grandfather.
Her family has deep South Boston roots, with her grandparents founding Joseph's Bakery in 1967 as community cornerstones.
Dennett runs to support The Ollie, an organization enhancing community life in South Boston through various programs.

title: She’s running Boston for her mom and cancer research
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Sophie Goad, from Burbank, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon for the Dana-Farber Cancer Institute.
Goad's fundraising supports the Claudia Adams Barr Program, contributing to Dana-Farber's $8.75 million team goal.
Her participation honors her mother, a cancer survivor, and aims to raise $15,124 for innovative cancer research.

title: Jaylen Brown says underwater training with a surfing legend helped him deal with anxiety
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Jaylen Brown credits underwater training with surfing legend Laird Hamilton for helping manage anxiety during high-pressure situations.
Brown's training involves simulating underwater conditions to learn relaxation techniques, aiding in his mental fortitude.
Brown had a standout season, averaging 28.8 points, 7.0 rebounds, and 5.2 assists, contributing to Celtics' playoff success.

title: Police search for owner, dog after man and dog are attacked in Everett
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Everett police seek a dog owner after a reported attack on a man and his dog on Ferry Street.
The injured dog, named Mushroom, required significant medical care, including surgery and 50 stitches.
Authorities are investigating to identify the owner who left before first responders arrived on the scene.

title: Mass. House passes bill restricting teen social media and phones in schools
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: The Massachusetts House passed a bill banning cellphones in schools and restricting social media access for those under 16.
The bill, passed 129-25, requires parental consent for social media use by 14- and 15-year-olds, blocking users under 14 entirely.
Social media companies must verify ages, and school districts need cellphone policies, subject to annual state review by Sept. 1.

title: How Boston.com readers feel about the Boston Marathon not making this list of best marathons
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: 77% of 98 Boston.com readers believe the Boston Marathon deserved a spot on USA Today's best marathons list.
Critics cited Boston Marathon's prestige, iconic course, and vibrant atmosphere as reasons for its deserved recognition.
Detractors labeled the event as elitist or overly commercialized, suggesting it lost its original competitive essence.

title: Increase in bear encounters prompts new requirements for White Mountains visitors
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: White Mountain National Forest requires overnight visitors to use bear-resistant canisters for food storage starting May 1.
The new rule targets the Pemigewasset Wilderness following a rise in bear encounters due to improperly stored food.
Free canister loans are available at multiple ranger stations, with penalties for non-compliance with the storage requirement.

title: Southbridge woman tried to hit teen with car while driving with daughter, police say
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Samantha Angers, 39, faces charges after allegedly trying to hit a teen with her car in Southbridge.
Police say Angers drove recklessly with her daughter, nearly hitting the teen multiple times on March 24.
Angers was charged with multiple offenses, including assault to kill, and held on $15,000 bail pending arraignment.

title: Industrial explosion sends multiple people to the hospital in East Providence
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Explosion at Aspen Aerogels plant in East Providence hospitalized 11 employees; all released by Thursday.
Incident occurred at 8:14 p.m., leading to a mass casualty declaration and regional emergency response.
Cause of explosion under investigation; Aspen Aerogels plans internal probe and shares updates as needed.
